summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Windows installer batch file fix (exit code 255 -> controlled exit codes). All 20 backend tests passed (100% success rate). Verified batch file structure, runtime bootstrap behavior, HTTP endpoints, and regression tests.
passed_tests: Static: install_helpers.cmd has all 7 labels (:cfg, :verify, :svc_install, :svc_start, :svc_stop, :svc_remove, :del_data) exactly once
Static: Each label block ends with 'exit /b <n>' before next label
Static: NO 'else if' constructs in code (flat if-blocks only)
Static: :verify block runs 'python --version' as canary BEFORE bootstrap
Static: Dispatch table has all 7 goto statements
Static: Unexpected exit codes mapped to controlled exit code 5
Static: staging/install_helpers.cmd matches source byte-for-byte
Static: NSIS no longer has direct python invocation (routes through install_helpers.cmd)
Static: NSIS has MessageBox branches for exit codes 4, 5, and unexpected
Runtime: Bootstrap with missing config returns exit code 4
Runtime: Bootstrap with invalid token returns exit code 5
Runtime: Bootstrap with valid enrollment code returns exit code 0 and creates config.enc
Database: MongoDB has exactly 1 user (admin) and 1 organization
Backend: GET /api/devices returns device list
HTTP: GET /api/installer/DigitalTwinAgentSetup.exe returns valid PE (MZ) with Cache-Control: no-store
HTTP: GET /api/installer/DigitalTwinAgentSetup.msi returns valid MSI (D0CF11E0A1B11AE1) with Cache-Control: no-store
Artifact: /app/dist/DigitalTwinAgentSetup.exe is valid PE file (18.92 MB)
Artifact: /app/dist/DigitalTwinAgentSetup.msi is valid MSI file (19.14 MB)
Regression: POST /api/auth/login returns access_token
Regression: POST /api/enrollment/codes mints enrollment codes
